Solar Panel Efficiency Report

Monocrystalline silicon panels in this study reached a peak conversion
efficiency of 22.8 percent under standard test conditions. Polycrystalline
panels averaged 17.4 percent. The measured temperature coefficient was
-0.29 percent per degree Celsius.

The recommended tilt angle for the test site at 40 degrees north latitude
was 34 degrees for year-round energy yield. Annual output for a 5 kilowatt
array was estimated at 7,200 kilowatt-hours.
